title: Why would loss go down while eval quality gets worse?
prompt:
Why would loss go down while eval quality gets worse?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
This is overfitting — the model memorizes training examples (loss decreases) but loses generalization ability (eval quality drops). It can also signal a mismatch between your loss function and actual quality: cross-entropy treats all tokens equally, but some tokens matter much more than others for answer quality.

**Takeaway:** Loss is a proxy, not the goal. Monitor eval metrics alongside loss. Use early stopping.
hint_notes: Loss is a proxy, not the goal. Monitor eval metrics alongside loss. Use early stopping.
